title: malveopsis subtriflora
intro: Malveopsis subtriflora is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Malvaceae family. It is native to the Mediterranean region and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Malveopsis subtriflora can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions.
intro_study: According to "Phytotherapy research : PTR", malveopsis subtriflora benefits include having rather moderate antimicrobial activity, high anti-inflammatory and wound healing activities, strong antioxidant activity, and anticancer properties.
